questiontext.text:
Measures of Central Tendency (Real Life Data)

Below are salaries of {@N@} employees in Bidco Africa company in Kenyan Shillings.

The recorded salaries are shown below:

\({@raw_data@}\)

Using the data above, answer the following questions.

(a) Calculate the mean monthly salary.

Mean = [[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]]

(b) Determine the median monthly salary.

Median = [[input:ans2]] [[validation:ans2]]

(c) Determine the mode of the salaries.

Mode = [[input:ans3]] [[validation:ans3]]

[[feedback:prt1]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked solution

Central tendency describes the value that represents the centre of a dataset. The three most common measures are the mean, median, and mode.

1. Mean

The mean is calculated by dividing the sum of all values by the number of observations. That is \[ \text{Mean} = \frac{\sum x}{N} \]

For this dataset:

Total number of employees: {@N@}

Correct Mean:

{@mean_val@} KES

---

2. Median

The median is the middle value when the data is arranged in ascending order.

Since the dataset contains {@N@} values (an odd number), the median position is:

\[ \frac{N+1}{2} = \frac{@N@+1}{2} \]

Correct Median:

{@median_val@} KES

---

3. Mode

The mode is the value that appears most frequently in the dataset.

The table below shows the frequency of each salary. The mode is highlighted.

{@freq_table_rows@}
Salary (KES) Frequency

Therefore, the Mode is:

{@ta_mode@} KES

---

Interpretation

  • Mean gives the overall average salary.
  • Median represents the middle salary when ordered.
  • Mode shows the most common salary in the company.

These three measures help statisticians understand how income or other real-world data is distributed.

questionnote.text: QN: sconcat( "CentralTendencyData | N=", string(N), " | data=", string(raw_data), " | mean=", string(ta_mean), " | median=", string(ta_median), " | mode=", string(ta_mode) );

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
